query_normalized: naturebench: can coding agents match the published sota of nature-family papers? linguistics
rationale: The paper focuses on a specific NLP application (automatic multiple-choice question generation) and does not address the user's core question regarding the performance of coding agents against SOTA benchmarks in the "Nature" family of papers or the specific domain of linguistics. It fails to meet any inclusion criteria (a-f) as it studies a different task, different agents, and a different evaluation context without establishing a baseline or mechanism relevant to coding agent capabilities.
